summary: New feature (attendance change approval) — 16/18 pytest passed. Two backend bugs discovered: (1) CRITICAL route-ordering bug: /attendance-changes/batch/{approve,reject} are eclipsed by the earlier /attendance-changes/{req_id}/{approve,reject} routes → batch calls return 500 InvalidId('batch'). (2) HIGH: PUT /attendance/{record_id}/status uses direct list membership `'edit_attendance' in user_permissions` and does NOT special-case admin → admin (whose permissions list has 43 entries but NOT edit_attendance) receives 403. Feature spec requires ADMIN bypass. All other flows (E2E pending→approve/reject, faculty scope, self-cancel, inside-window direct write, duplicate-replaces-pending, pending-count badge, dept-head triggers pending, sibling dept-head cannot approve) work correctly.

critical.issue: Route registration order in /app/backend/backend/routes/attendance_approval.py places @router.post('/attendance-changes/{req_id}/approve') (L315) and '/{req_id}/reject' (L357) BEFORE '/batch/approve' (L395) and '/batch/reject' (L436). FastAPI matches by order, so 'batch' is captured as req_id → ObjectId('batch') → 500 InvalidId. Fix: move the two /batch/* endpoints ABOVE the two /{req_id}/* endpoints (or rename to /attendance-changes/batch-approve).

server.py line 9013-9016 uses `has_edit_perm = 'edit_attendance' in user_permissions` and rejects with 403 if False. Admin's permissions list does NOT contain 'edit_attendance' (confirmed via /api/auth/login → 43 perms, no edit_attendance). Result: admin cannot use this endpoint at all — contradicting the feature spec (‘ADMIN مستثنى — تطبيق مباشر بدون pending’). Fix: replace check with `has_permission(current_user, 'edit_attendance')` (which handles admin auto-yes) OR add early `if current_user['role'] == UserRole.ADMIN: has_edit_perm = True`.

minor.issue: RejectPayload is required in the signature (no default), so callers MUST send a JSON body even when review_notes is None. Consider making it Optional[RejectPayload] = None to allow empty POSTs.
No permission check — any authenticated user can list pending requests for any lecture (potential info leak). Consider gating on view_attendance / faculty scope.
No filter by faculty; harmless, but does not verify requested_by == user['id'] with any secondary constraint. Fine as-is but worth noting.

action_items: Reorder attendance_approval.py routes: place @router.post('/attendance-changes/batch/approve') and '/batch/reject' BEFORE the '/{req_id}/approve' and '/{req_id}/reject' handlers so FastAPI matches 'batch' literally instead of as a path parameter. Currently batch calls always return 500.

Fix admin bypass in PUT /attendance/{record_id}/status (server.py ~L9013): change to `has_edit_perm = has_permission(current_user, 'edit_attendance')` so admin (role==admin) is auto-allowed. Without this, admin cannot edit attendance from this endpoint at all.
(Optional) Add permission gate to GET /attendance-changes/lecture/{lecture_id}/pending to prevent cross-faculty information disclosure.
critical_code_review_comments: attendance_approval.py L189-193 (_can_approve): correctly delegates to has_permission which auto-allows admin. Good.
attendance_approval.py L263-264 & L332-334 (faculty scope): dean without faculty_id would see ALL pending — acceptable since seed data uses faculty_id, but consider explicit guard.

attendance_approval.py L96-107 (auto-cancel previous pending on duplicate): elegant idempotency but does NOT enforce a unique index on (lecture_id, student_id, status='pending'). A concurrent race could still create two pending. Consider a partial index: db.attendance_change_requests.create_index([('lecture_id',1),('student_id',1)], partialFilterExpression={'status':'pending'}, unique=True).
attendance_approval.py L395-433 & L436-470: 90% duplicate code between batch_approve and batch_reject; refactor into a helper.

server.py L8843: uses `current_user['role'] not in [UserRole.ADMIN, UserRole.DEAN]` — coupled to role string; if a custom role gets APPROVE_ATTENDANCE_CHANGES but is not literally dean, it will still be forced through the approval mechanism. Consider checking `_can_approve(current_user)` instead.
server.py L9032: same coupling concern for PUT endpoint.
server.py L15671-15678: migration branch only adds APPROVE_ATTENDANCE_CHANGES if system_key == 'dean'. Admin does not need it because _can_approve short-circuits. OK.
attendance_approval.py L473-496 cancel_request: fine, but does not check faculty_id — however since requester ownership is checked, this is safe.

attendance_approval.py L196-233 _apply_change_to_attendance: when the original attendance row was deleted between request and approval, it inserts a new row — good. But the new row uses `date=now` (UTC) while normal flow uses YEMEN_TZ; minor consistency issue.
test_credentials: admin/admin123 used. Test users (TEST_APPR_*) auto-seeded via pymongo with bcrypt, torn down at module end.
seed_data_creation: All docs seeded/removed per-test-module. TAG=TEST_APPR_<uuid>. Cleanup fixture in test file removes: attendance_change_requests (by faculty_id), attendance (by course_id), lectures (by course_id), students/courses/departments/faculties (by TAG regex), users (by TAG regex). DB verified clean after run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Regression file is /app/backend/tests/test_attendance_approval.py. To re-run after the two fixes: `pytest /app/backend/tests/test_attendance_approval.py -v`. The two currently-failing tests (test_step9_admin_direct_apply_outside_window, test_step11_batch_approve_and_reject) will pass once the main agent (a) reorders routes so /batch/* is registered before /{req_id}/* and (b) uses has_permission() (or admin role early-return) inside PUT /attendance/{record_id}/status. All other 16 tests already validate the happy paths + edge cases described in the review request.
rca of the issue: Route-ordering: FastAPI resolves routes in decoration order. Registering /{req_id}/approve before /batch/approve makes 'batch' get captured as a path parameter; ObjectId('batch') then raises InvalidId and FastAPI surfaces it as 500. Admin 403: the endpoint pre-dates the new approval feature and its permission check is a raw list membership, not the has_permission() helper that grants admin auto-yes. Both are one-line fixes.
